content: Omega-7 fatty acids are monounsaturated fats found in cold water fish and macadamia nuts.
Omega-7 fatty acids, specifically pyic acid, are lipocines that control how other fats function in the body.
A randomized placebo study showed that pyic acid, an Omega-7 fatty acid, reduced C-reactive protein, triglycerides, LDL cholesterol, and increased HDL cholesterol in patients with dyslipidemia.
Pyic acid, an Omega-7 fatty acid, prevents beta cell apoptosis in insulin-producing cells, improving insulin utilization and reducing fat storage.
Studies indicate that Omega-7 fatty acids, particularly pyic acid, can reduce appetite by increasing the production of the satiety hormone kosyainin.
Consuming fatty fish provides both Omega-3 and Omega-7 fatty acids, potentially enhancing the effects of Omega-3 supplementation.
